examples: Colour has a profound, though often subliminal, influence on our senses and moods.
Such sentences do not always occur in thinking, and when they do they are merged with vague imagery and the hint of unconscious or subliminal activities going on just out of range.
And yes, the unconscious effects of subliminal images may be a variant of blindsight, although the jury remains out on just how similar the two phenomena are.
The researchers speculated that dirty money acts as a subliminal prime towards dodgy dealing and ill-gotten gains.
There's at least a subliminal link to Mary Queen of Scots, who was born in the palace.
